High Court on 07.11.2024, that while making recruitment to judicial 
service, they ought to provide separate qualifying marks for PwBD 
in the Preliminary and Main Examinations. This Court also observed 
that the qualifying marks should ordinarily be the same as for SC/
ST candidates or can even be lower if so prescribed by the relevant 
Rules. If the Rules are silent, then the competent authority can lay 
down such qualifying marks.
56. It is also pertinent to point out at this juncture that pursuant to the order 
of this Court dated 07.11.2024, the Delhi High Court has been providing 
reservation for persons with benchmark disabilities on reserved post 
in judicial service. Further, the copy of the minutes of the meeting of 
Examination Committee held on 11.12.2024, proceeds to state that the 
order passed by this Court issuing directions governing the selection 
of candidates to the District Judiciary across the country has been
